question: Will Kirk Cousins play for Seattle Seahawks in 2026-27?
description:
This market will resolve to the next team Kirk Cousins officially joins by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

If Kirk Cousins does not officially join a new team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Kirk Cousins joins a team that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Kirk Cousins is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional team by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

An official signing announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from the NFL and/or the acquiring team. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Falcons' midseason benching of Kirk Cousins in favor of rookie Michael Penix Jr. has cratered Atlanta's retention odds to 5%, positioning the veteran quarterback as a prime 2026 free agent amid his $180 million contract's team-friendly exit ramps post-2025. Raiders lead trader consensus at 22% thanks to their persistent QB void after inconsistent play from Aidan O'Connell and Gardner Minshew, bolstered by cap space and new regime appeal. Packers (20.5%) and Bills (18.4%) trail closely, reflecting speculation on Jordan Love's extension risks and Josh Allen contingency planning, respectively, in a crowded field of 30+ suitors where proven pocket passers like Cousins draw bids from contenders eyeing stability over splashy drafts.

question: Will Duke advance to the Elite Eight?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team advances to the Elite Eight of the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to advance to the Elite Eight of the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament (e.g. they are eliminated, withdraws, disqualified), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament Elite Eight matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for NCAA Tournament teams reaching the Elite Eight heavily favors blue-blood programs like Duke and Purdue, implied probabilities reflecting their top seeds, star power from Cooper Flagg and Zach Edey, and historically easier paths in loaded brackets. Recent Sweet 16 upsets, including NC State's Cinderella run past higher seeds, have boosted underdogs like the Wolfpack to 15-20% chances in some markets, while injuries to key guards on Houston and Tennessee tempered their odds. Momentum from dominant second-week wins, rest advantages for Friday games, and matchup edges against fatigued opponents shape shifting lines, underscoring the tournament's volatility where bracket busts routinely defy chalk. Upcoming regional semifinals will test depth amid rising foul trouble risks.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to advance to the Elite Eight of the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament (e.g. they are eliminated, withdraws, disqualified), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament Elite Eight matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus for NCAA Tournament teams reaching the Elite Eight heavily favors blue-blood programs like Duke and Purdue, implied probabilities reflecting their top seeds, star power from Cooper Flagg and Zach Edey, and historically easier paths in loaded brackets. Recent Sweet 16 upsets, including NC State's Cinderella run past higher seeds, have boosted underdogs like the Wolfpack to 15-20% chances in some markets, while injuries to key guards on Houston and Tennessee tempered their odds. Momentum from dominant second-week wins, rest advantages for Friday games, and matchup edges against fatigued opponents shape shifting lines, underscoring the tournament's volatility where bracket busts routinely defy chalk. Upcoming regional semifinals will test depth amid rising foul trouble risks.

question: Spread: Al Shabab Saudi Club (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for April 10 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Al Shabab Saudi Club" if Al Shabab Saudi Club win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Al Ittihad Saudi Club".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on slstat.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.
outcomes: ["Al Shabab Saudi Club", "Al Ittihad Saudi Club"]

events.title: Al Shabab Saudi Club vs. Al Ittihad Saudi Club - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for April 10 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Shabab's solid home form at Prince Faisal bin Fahd Stadium anchors trader consensus in this Saudi Pro League clash, with recent 2-1 wins over mid-table foes boosting their momentum against an Al Ittihad side mired in a three-match skid. Ittihad's absentee stars, including Karim Benzema (hamstring doubt per official report) and N'Golo Kanté (suspension risk), expose defensive frailties evident in conceding 8 goals last three outings. Head-to-head favors Al Shabab (3 wins in last 5), while Ittihad's travel fatigue from AFC Champions League adds pressure. Markets price Al Shabab edge amid uncertainty over lineup confirmations.

events.description: More markets for the Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for April 10 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al-Najma's strong home form at Prince Sultan bin Abdulaziz Stadium drives trader consensus toward them in markets surrounding their Saudi Pro League clash with struggling NEOM SC, with implied probabilities reflecting about 65% favoritism for a home win. question: Will Duke advance to the Final Four?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team advances to the semifinals of the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to advance to the semifinals of the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament (e.g. they are eliminated, withdraws, disqualified), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament semifinals matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Duke leads trader consensus to reach the NCAA Tournament semifinals, with implied probabilities around 17% driven by Cooper Flagg's dominant freshman impact and a deep roster under Jon Scheyer, fresh off early-season wins over ranked foes like Arizona. Kansas trails closely at ~14%, bolstered by Hunter Dickinson's return but tempered by a tough Big 12 slate ahead. Recent developments favor Duke after no major injuries in their non-conference run, while UConn's rebuild post-dynasty introduces uncertainty. Historical Final Four volatility—upsets like NC State's Cinderella run last year—highlights schedule strength and March momentum as key upcoming factors shaping odds.

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to advance to the semifinals of the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament (e.g. they are eliminated, withdraws, disqualified), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament is cancelled, postponed after April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament semifinals matchups have not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Duke leads trader consensus to reach the NCAA Tournament semifinals, with implied probabilities around 17% driven by Cooper Flagg's dominant freshman impact and a deep roster under Jon Scheyer, fresh off early-season wins over ranked foes like Arizona. Kansas trails closely at ~14%, bolstered by Hunter Dickinson's return but tempered by a tough Big 12 slate ahead. Recent developments favor Duke after no major injuries in their non-conference run, while UConn's rebuild post-dynasty introduces uncertainty. Historical Final Four volatility—upsets like NC State's Cinderella run last year—highlights schedule strength and March momentum as key upcoming factors shaping odds.

question: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns low implied probability to Kharg Island—the Persian Gulf's vital Iranian oil export hub—losing Tehran's control by the resolution date, reflecting its firm operational status amid Israel-Iran shadow war tensions. Recent Israeli airstrikes in October 2024 hit fuel depots near Tehran but bypassed Kharg, while Houthi threats to Gulf shipping lanes have not disrupted Iranian oversight there. No verified intelligence or official reports signal imminent challenges to sovereignty, prioritizing the island's economic lifeline role. Key risks stem from escalation in proxy conflicts or direct reprisals, with traders monitoring U.S. diplomatic signals and IAEA nuclear updates for potential market movers.

question: Total Kills Over/Under 45.5 in Game 2?
description:
In the upcoming Upper bracket final match between BetBoom Team and Team Yandex in the PGL Wallachia Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 14 at 10:00AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 46 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Dota 2: BetBoom Team vs Team Yandex (BO3) - PGL Wallachia Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Upper bracket final match between BetBoom Team and Team Yandex in the PGL Wallachia Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 14 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "BetBoom Team" if BetBoom Team win the match against Team Yandex.

This market will resolve to "Team Yandex" if Team Yandex win the match against BetBoom Team.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Will Khamenei post 20-39 posts from March 17 to March 24, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Khamenei (@khamenei_ir), posts on X between March 17, 12:00 PM ET and March 24, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Ayatollah Khamenei's official X account (@khamenei_ir) posting volume from March 17-24, 2026, centers on 40-59 posts (56.3% implied probability) and 20-39 posts (34%), reflecting the account's historical average of 5-8 posts daily over recent months, including statements, quotes, and media shares. This baseline, drawn from consistent activity amid Iran's geopolitical tensions and domestic events, drives sentiment, with higher ranges unlikely absent major escalations. Recent developments show steady output post-2024 presidential election and regional conflicts, unaltered by unverified health rumors. The period overlaps Nowruz holidays (starting March 20), potentially slowing posts and reinforcing moderate outcomes, though traders acknowledge uncertainty from unscheduled announcements.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Ayatollah Khamenei's official X account (@khamenei_ir) posting volume from March 17-24, 2026, centers on 40-59 posts (56.3% implied probability) and 20-39 posts (34%), reflecting the account's historical average of 5-8 posts daily over recent months, including statements, quotes, and media shares. This baseline, drawn from consistent activity amid Iran's geopolitical tensions and domestic events, drives sentiment, with higher ranges unlikely absent major escalations. Recent developments show steady output post-2024 presidential election and regional conflicts, unaltered by unverified health rumors. The period overlaps Nowruz holidays (starting March 20), potentially slowing posts and reinforcing moderate outcomes, though traders acknowledge uncertainty from unscheduled announcements.

question: Will Córdoba CF win on 2026-03-27?
description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for March 27, 2026
If Córdoba CF wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Córdoba CF vs. CD Mirandés
events.description: This event is for the upcoming La Liga 2 game, scheduled for Friday, March 27, 2026 between Córdoba CF and CD Mirandés.

eventMetadata.context_description: Córdoba CF's unbeaten home streak in LaLiga Hypermotion meets CD Mirandés' solid away resilience, fueling the razor-thin trader consensus where Mirandés edges at 50%, Córdoba at 49%, and draw at 48.5%. question: Who will finish higher: Colapinto or Gasly?
description:
This market will resolve according to the driver who finishes ahead of the other at the F1 Bahrain Grand Prix, scheduled for Apr 12, 2026.

If Colapinto finishes ahead of Gasly, this market will resolve to "Colapinto".
If Gasly finishes ahead of Colapinto, this market will resolve to "Gasly".

In the case of a tie between two drivers, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the Bahrain Grand Prix is postponed this market will remain open until the vent has been completed. If the Bahrain Grand Prix is permanently canceled this market will resolve 50-50.

If either driver fails to finish the race (including DNF, DSQ, or any other non-finish), the driver who completes more laps will be considered to have finished ahead. If both drivers complete the same number of laps, this market will resolve 50-50, regardless of the order in which they retired. This rule applies even if one or both drivers are unclassified in the FIA “Final Classification.”

This market will resolve based on the "Final Classification" published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The "Final Classification" is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the "Final Classification" will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

events.title: Bahrain Grand Prix: Head-to-Head
events.description: Head-to-head markets for the 2026 F1 Bahrain Grand Prix.

eventMetadata.context_description: Red Bull's historical edge at the Bahrain International Circuit anchors trader sentiment in the head-to-head market, with Max Verstappen securing victory in the last three editions amid the track's emphasis on straightline speed and tire management. Recent pre-season testing in Bahrain highlighted McLaren's pace in long runs, boosting Lando Norris' implied probability against Verstappen, while Ferrari's upgrades narrowed the gap for Charles Leclerc in similar matchups. Momentum from McLaren's late-2024 surge tempers Red Bull's favoritism, but qualifying performance—crucial on Sakhir's abrasive surface—remains the pivotal swing factor ahead of the April 13 night race, where DRS zones favor bold overtakers. Upsets are rare but possible given evolving car balances.

events.title: Bahrain Grand Prix: Head-to-Head
events.description: Head-to-head markets for the 2026 F1 Bahrain Grand Prix.

eventMetadata.context_description: Red Bull's historical edge at the Bahrain International Circuit anchors trader sentiment in the head-to-head market, with Max Verstappen securing victory in the last three editions amid the track's emphasis on straightline speed and tire management. Momentum from McLaren's late-2024 surge tempers Red Bull's favoritism, but qualifying performance—crucial on Sakhir's abrasive surface—remains the pivotal swing factor ahead of the April 13 night race, where DRS zones favor bold overtakers. Upsets are rare but possible given evolving car balances.

events.title: Bahrain Grand Prix: Head-to-Head
events.description: Head-to-head markets for the 2026 F1 Bahrain Grand Prix.

eventMetadata.context_description: Red Bull's historical edge at the Bahrain International Circuit anchors trader sentiment in the head-to-head market, with Max Verstappen securing victory in the last three editions amid the track's emphasis on straightline speed and tire management. Recent pre-season testing in Bahrain highlighted McLaren's pace in long runs, boosting Lando Norris' implied probability against Verstappen, while Ferrari's upgrades narrowed the gap for Charles Leclerc in similar matchups. Momentum from McLaren's late-2024 surge tempers Red Bull's favoritism, but qualifying performance—crucial on Sakhir's abrasive surface—remains the pivotal swing factor ahead of the April 13 night race, where DRS zones favor bold overtakers. Upsets are rare but possible given evolving car balances.

question: Who will finish higher: Piastri or Russell?
description:
This market will resolve according to the driver who finishes ahead of the other at the F1 Bahrain Grand Prix, scheduled for Apr 12, 2026.

If Piastri finishes ahead of Russell, this market will resolve to "Piastri".
If Russell finishes ahead of Piastri, this market will resolve to "Russell".

In the case of a tie between two drivers, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the Bahrain Grand Prix is postponed this market will remain open until the vent has been completed. If the Bahrain Grand Prix is permanently canceled this market will resolve 50-50.

If either driver fails to finish the race (including DNF, DSQ, or any other non-finish), the driver who completes more laps will be considered to have finished ahead. If both drivers complete the same number of laps, this market will resolve 50-50, regardless of the order in which they retired. This rule applies even if one or both drivers are unclassified in the FIA “Final Classification.”

This market will resolve based on the "Final Classification" published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race. The "Final Classification" is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the "Final Classification" will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed driver finishes within the top three positions in the official "Final Classification" for the F1 Bahrain Grand Prix, scheduled for Apr 12, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The "Final Classification" is published by the FIA following the conclusion of the race and includes any applied time penalties and official adjustments. It is typically released 30-60 minutes after the race ends.

Disqualifications or changes made after the publication of the "Final Classification" will not affect market resolution. The timing of the podium ceremony does not determine the result for this market — only the FIA's published classification will be used to resolve this market.

If the Bahrain Grand Prix is postponed, this market will remain open until the event has been completed. If the Bahrain Grand Prix is permanently canceled, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Bahrain Grand Prix podium markets heavily favors Max Verstappen and Charles Leclerc, with implied probabilities above 70% reflecting Red Bull and Ferrari's strong pre-season testing pace on the abrasive Sakhir circuit. Recent developments include McLaren's upgraded MCL39 showing improved long-run speed in Bahrain testing, boosting Lando Norris to around 50% odds, while Mercedes lags due to ongoing power unit issues. Key factors shaping sentiment: track's emphasis on tire management and overtaking, mild weather forecast minimizing chaos, and Verstappen's perfect Bahrain podium record since 2022. Watch Friday practice for setup tweaks and any surprise reliability hiccups, as upsets like Piastri's potential rise could shift odds.

events.title: FC Twente '65 vs. FC Volendam
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for Friday, April 10, 2026 between FC Twente '65 and FC Volendam.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment reflects razor-thin margins in the FC Twente '65 vs. FC Volendam matchup, with all outcomes hovering at 48.5% implied probability amid evenly matched form in the Dutch lower divisions. FC Twente '65 holds a slight home edge but has drawn three of its last five league games, exposing defensive vulnerabilities against counterattacks that suit Volendam's resilient backline. Volendam, fresh off a gritty 1-1 draw versus a top-table rival, boasts momentum from two wins in four, though key midfielder Jordy Hartog remains sidelined per the latest injury report. Head-to-head history shows two draws in recent encounters, underscoring low-scoring stalemates; upcoming weather forecasts predict rain, further tilting toward caution and tightening the wisdom-of-crowds pricing.

events.title: FC Utrecht vs. Telstar 1963
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for Saturday, April 11, 2026 between FC Utrecht and Telstar 1963.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Utrecht enters this KNVB Cup clash as a narrow favorite despite their Eredivisie pedigree, but Telstar's impressive Eerste Divisie run—unbeaten in five with high-scoring wins—fuels trader skepticism, compressing odds to a near deadlock. Recent Utrecht stumbles, including a 2-1 league loss to Heracles and key midfielder Jens Toornstra sidelined by hamstring strain per official reports, erode their edge, while Telstar boasts full squad availability and cup pedigree from last season's quarterfinal push. description:

  This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-19 in ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.

If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.

events.title: ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - More Markets
events.description: This market refers to the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-19 in ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Sri Lanka in the ODI series against Afghanistan, with markets pricing the hosts at around 65% implied probability for victory after their 2-0 lead following dominant wins in the first two matches. Key driver: Sri Lanka's balanced attack led by Wanindu Hasaranga's spin (8 wickets across games) exploiting Afghanistan's middle-order frailties, while Pathum Nissanka's consistent form bolsters the top order. Afghanistan struggles without Rashid Khan at full fitness—officially nursing a back issue per team reports—limiting their spin threat. Upcoming third ODI in Colombo offers Afghanistan a consolation shot, but Sri Lanka's home advantage and rest edge maintain sentiment skewed toward a clean sweep. Recent rain delays add minor uncertainty to pitch behavior.

question: ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka: Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka - Team Top Batter Afghanistan Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Afghanistan and Sri Lanka scheduled for 2026-03-19 in ODI Series Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Afghanistan will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Afghanistan.
The outcome corresponding to Sri Lanka will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Sri Lanka.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin race for team top batter supremacy in the Afghanistan-Sri Lanka ODI, with draw odds leading at 48.5% amid evenly matched lineups and unpredictable Dambulla pitch conditions favoring spin. Afghanistan's Ibrahim Zadran (recent 162*) and Rahmanullah Gurbaz anchor a potent top order, while Sri Lanka counters with Pathum Nissanka's consistency (series avg 45+) and Charith Asalanka's middle-order firepower. First ODI saw Nissanka top-score with 65, but Zadran's explosive potential keeps AFG viable at 47.5%; LKA edges at 47.0% on home advantage and spin-friendly tracks. Momentum tilts slightly to visitors after SL's bowling dominance, yet Afghanistan's home form and batting depth sustain the deadlock.

First ODI saw Nissanka top-score with 65, but Zadran's explosive potential keeps AFG viable at 47.5%; LKA edges at 47.0% on home advantage and spin-friendly tracks. Momentum tilts slightly to visitors after SL's bowling dominance, yet Afghanistan's home form and batting depth sustain the deadlock.

question: Legends Cricket League: Konark Suryas Odisha vs India Tigers
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Konark Suryas Odisha and India Tigers scheduled for March 20 2026 in Legends Cricket League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

description:

If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.
